value_metric: a rehearsed, deterministic backup-restore proves a 2016 month, a 2020 month, and the dense current month all recover from the open permanent evidence into an isolated copy with body/attachment/relationship/counts intact, and the retention matrix guarantees original official evidence and published versions are never deleted -- so the 2016+ history is genuinely recoverable, not just archived

cost_itemized_unknown_not_zero.human_maintenance: offline restore drill into a throwaway in-memory SQLite; production D1/R2 never touched; real restores would run against a spare D1 under the same runbook

finding: single-month isolated restore orphaned 1 version whose parent document was first seen in an earlier month; fixed by making restore referentially closed (pull each version's parent document across partitions) -> 0 orphans
